name: Luckey Roberts
abstract: Charles Luckyth Roberts (August 7, 1887 – February 5, 1968), better known as Luckey Roberts, was an American composer and stride pianist who worked in the jazz, ragtime, and blues styles. Roberts performed as musician, band/orchestra conductor, and dancer.

concerts.label: Benefit: Crescendo Club for Destitute Negro Songwriters

about_quotes.speaker: Danny Barker
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah, I played with Luckey Roberts. I played with Lucky Roberts, me and Luckey Roberts and Sonny Thompson.

about_quotes.speaker: Danny Barker
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah, I played with Luckey Roberts. I played with Lucky Roberts, me and Luckey Roberts and Sonny Thompson.

about_quotes.speaker: Freddie Green
about_quotes.evidence: I often wondered, who's Lucky Roberts? ... Well, I never had a chance, really, to hear Roberts.

about_quotes.speaker: Bud Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: I was very much influenced by Lucky Roberts, the great black pianist

about_quotes.speaker: Monk Montgomery
about_quotes.evidence: I was Lucky Roberts -- in the '30's I played with Lucky Roberts.

about_quotes.speaker: Jean Bach
about_quotes.evidence: I found him, because I put an ad in the Amsterdam News... And one was the little boy sitting next to Basie... But the other person that decided to sit down was Willie the Lion Smith. And he’s so big and pompous and arrogant you know. And he was standing there with this man who is half his size, Luck
about_quotes.doc_title: Jean Bach interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995
